2026-04-16 21:51 · 9,141 stocks · 196 API calls
Lynch + Buffett
🤖 AI Top Picks — Claude Sonnet
1. DLO DLocal Limited · Technology / Financial Services HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower

"The only payment rail that actually works in Nigeria, Bangladesh, and Egypt — and Wall Street refuses to model it"

DLocal is the sole scaled local-to-local payment network covering 40+ high-friction emerging markets, processing payments for the world's biggest global merchants (Amazon, Uber, Spotify) where Stripe and PayPal simply do not operate. The market prices DLO as a high-risk FinTech with lingering 2023 governance overhang, but ROIC of 29%, FCF margin of 37%, and 47% revenue growth — on top of 15% the prior year — prove the business is compounding at institutional quality. At PEG 0.9 with 84% DCF margin of safety, this is the fastest-growing name in the list at the cheapest earnings multiple.

Price $13.47 PEG 0.9 P/E 20.3 ROIC 28.5% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings (expected July 2026) showing continued 40%+ revenue growth and margin stability would force analyst coverage upgrades from the minimal institutional base currently covering the stock; any governance-related overhang resolution or major new merchant contract announcement would re-rate the stock immediately. Watch EM currency crisis or dollar strengthening compressing TPV take rates across key markets (Nigeria, Argentina) — this is the single thesis-breaking risk and must be monitored quarterly via FX sensitivity disclosures.
2. DOCS Doximity, Inc. · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Fast Grower

"The LinkedIn for doctors — 80% of US physicians already use it, the stock trades at 32% of its high, and pharma hasn't even started paying full price yet"

Doximity owns the only verified professional network of US physicians (80%+ penetration, secured by medical license verification) and monetizes it through pharma marketing and telehealth workflows — a captive audience that cannot be replicated. The market sold the stock to 32% of its 52-week high on a pharma spend slowdown that appears fully priced, while missing that revenue just re-accelerated from 13% to 20% and FCF margins of 49% with zero debt make this a fortress. The market treats it as a COVID-era beneficiary in decline — it is actually a durable platform in early-innings monetization.

Price $24.32 PEG 1.6 P/E 18.7 ROIC 20.5% Catalyst H1 2026 pharma marketing budget recovery — major pharma companies are finalizing 2026 ad budgets with GLP-1 launches driving record physician outreach spend; Q1 FY2027 earnings (expected May 2026) guidance raise would be the specific trigger for re-rating from 32% of 52-week high. Watch Pharma consolidation causing marketing budget compression or a shift to direct physician engagement channels that bypass platform advertising — any structural change in how pharma reaches physicians is the thesis-breaker.
3. DUOL Duolingo, Inc. · Technology HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Turnaround

"600 million users building daily language habits, 39% revenue growth, FCF margins of 36% — and the stock sits at 19% of its 52-week high because everyone still thinks it's a free app"

Duolingo is mispriced as a consumer app when it is actually a subscription platform with 100M+ daily active users locked in by one of the most powerful behavioral mechanisms in consumer tech — the streak. With 39% revenue growth for two consecutive years, 72% gross margins, and FCF margins of 36%, the unit economics have been proven, yet trailing PE of 11x and PEG of 0.7 price in zero execution credit. The market missed the transition: Duolingo is not a free app — Super Duolingo and Max tier conversions are driving ARPU expansion at scale, and the AI personalization engine makes the product measurably better every quarter.

Price $103.39 PEG 0.7 P/E 11.1 ROIC 9.4% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings (expected August 2026) showing Super Duolingo/Max subscriber growth and ARPU expansion above consensus — the AI tutor integration is a specific product upgrade driving 2-3x pricing power over standard tiers; any DAU monetization data point above expectations is the trigger. Watch AI-native language learning competitors (OpenAI, Google, specialized tutoring bots) offering superior personalization at lower cost — if a well-funded entrant (Google's AI tutor in Assistant/Chrome) commoditizes basic language instruction, the willingness-to-pay for Duolingo Max erodes.
4. MNDY monday.com Ltd. · Technology HIGH WITHIN MONTHS Turnaround

"A work-OS platform beating Asana and Smartsheet with 89% gross margins and a forward PE of 8.2x — trading at 21% of its 52-week high because it hasn't proved operating leverage yet, and it is about to"

monday.com is not a project management tool — it is a no-code application builder where companies reconstruct their core business processes on top of the platform, creating switching costs that make churn economically painful. The market conflates it with Asana and Smartsheet (commoditizing tools) but MNDY has already won the SMB-to-mid-market segment and is extending into CRM, Dev, and Service modules. The forward PE of 8.2x vs trailing 28.5x signals an earnings step-change the market has not credited — ROIC turning positive is the precise inflection point where the growth-stock-without-profits label dissolves.

Price $65.45 PEG 1.6 P/E 28.5 ROIC -0.1% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings showing back-to-back positive operating margins would trigger an immediate re-rating from growth-stock-with-thin-margins to profitable-compounder — a single quarter of confirmed operating leverage closing the fwd PE to trailing PE gap from 71% is the specific event to watch. Watch Microsoft Power Platform or Salesforce Flow deepening enterprise penetration with bundled pricing that undercuts monday.com's standalone value proposition — if the large-cap suite vendors bundle work-OS functionality into existing ELA agreements, pricing power erodes.
5. PEGA Pegasystems Inc. · Technology HIGH WITHIN WEEKS Quality Compounder

"Enterprise AI workflow automation with 29% ROIC and revenue re-accelerating from 5% to 17% — the market hasn't noticed the earnings upgrade cycle has already started"

Pega builds the AI decisioning and process automation platforms that banks, insurers, and telecoms run their core customer operations on — multi-year, multi-million-dollar deployments with contractual switching costs that make replacement a board-level decision. Revenue just re-accelerated sharply from 5% to 17%, signaling that the cloud transition from perpetual license to SaaS recurring revenue is inflecting profitability — yet the stock sits at 64% of its 52-week high with PEG 0.8 and ROIC 29%. The EG5y of 24% against a PEG of 0.8 is the clearest valuation anomaly in the enterprise software space.

Price $43.50 PEG 0.8 P/E 18.5 ROIC 28.9% Catalyst Next two earnings reports (Q2 and Q3 2026) where the revenue re-acceleration from 5% to 17% is confirmed and analyst estimate upgrades begin — the ⚠RevGap of +97% suggests analysts have dramatically underestimated the earnings inflection; each positive surprise compresses the PEG further and triggers institutional re-engagement. Watch ServiceNow or Salesforce acquiring a Pega competitor and bundling enterprise AI workflow at discount pricing — Pega's size ($7.4B market cap) makes it a potential M&A target but also vulnerable to being out-resourced in product development by much larger suite vendors.
6. QLYS Qualys, Inc. · Technology HIGH WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount

"A cloud-native security platform with 46% FCF margins, 28% ROIC, and a PE of 15.4 that the market prices like a melting business — it is the opposite"

Qualys runs the compliance and vulnerability management infrastructure that enterprises cannot turn off — if you rip out Qualys, you fail your SOC 2 audit, your PCI compliance review, and your cyber insurance renewal simultaneously. Despite 83% gross margins, 46% FCF margins, 28% ROIC, and 74% DCF margin of safety, the stock trades at PE 15.4 (half the Technology sector average) because 10% revenue growth reads as 'slow' to growth-focused analysts who miss that this is a royalty-like recurring SaaS stream with near-zero churn. This is a quality compounder hiding in plain sight, mispriced as an also-ran.

Price $83.43 PEG 1.6 P/E 15.4 ROIC 28.1% Catalyst Q2 2026 ARR re-acceleration from the DMARC/TotalCloud upsell cycle — as enterprises layer cloud-native workloads on top of on-premise Qualys deployments, the platform cross-sell drives ARR growth above the current 10% ceiling; any guidance raise above 12% revenue growth would force analyst re-rating from 'slow-growth' to 'durable compounder' classification. Watch Rapid consolidation by CrowdStrike or Microsoft Defender absorbing vulnerability management into XDR platforms at bundle pricing — if the market converges on a single security platform that commoditizes point solutions, Qualys's standalone value proposition weakens, though regulatory compliance mandates provide a structural floor.
7. HALO Halozyme Therapeutics · Healthcare HIGH WITHIN MONTHS IV Discount

"Roche, Pfizer, and J&J pay Halozyme royalties every time they sell a drug — and the market still prices this royalty machine as speculative biotech"

Halozyme's ENHANZE drug delivery technology converts intravenous drugs to subcutaneous injection, reducing hospital time and improving patient compliance — Roche, Pfizer, J&J, and Janssen license it and pay royalties on every unit sold. This is a royalty business with zero manufacturing risk, 82% gross margins, 58% operating margins, and 24% ROIC, yet the trailing PE of 25.3 vs forward PE of 7.5 signals that the royalty stream inflection is not yet in consensus models. The market classifies HALO as speculative biotech — it is actually one of the most capital-light royalty generators in healthcare.

Price $66.61 PEG 0.7 P/E 25.3 ROIC 23.6% Catalyst Forward PE of 7.5 vs trailing 25.3 collapses as royalty revenues from Darzalex SC (J&J's blockbuster, $11B+ annual sales) scale through 2026 — H1 2026 royalty disclosure showing Darzalex SC market share gains drives consensus EPS upgrades; any new partner drug approval using ENHANZE is an immediate re-rating event. Watch Partner drug failure or competitive displacement — if Darzalex SC loses market share to a new myeloma therapy that doesn't use ENHANZE, the royalty stream projections require downward revision; concentration risk in a small number of blockbuster partner drugs is the single biggest structural vulnerability.
8. HIG The Hartford Financial Services · Financial Services MEDIUM WITHIN MONTHS Quality Compounder

"An insurance compounder with 28% ROIC, 15% FCF yield, and PEG 0.8 near 52-week highs — the market thinks insurance is boring, which is exactly why it keeps compounding"

The Hartford compounds quietly — 28% ROIC, 15% FCF yield, consistent 7-8% revenue growth, PEG 0.8, and PE of 10.1 in a Financial Services sector trading at PEG 1.1. Commercial lines pricing power is sustaining above-loss-cost rate increases, and Hartford's workers compensation and specialty lines franchise is not a commodity business — it requires underwriting expertise accumulated over decades that cannot be disrupted by an app. Near 52-week highs, this is a business the market is correctly rewarding, and at PEG 0.8 it remains undervalued relative to its consistent earnings power.

Price $137.59 PEG 0.8 P/E 10.1 ROIC 28.2% Catalyst Q2 2026 earnings (July 2026) showing continued combined ratio improvement and commercial lines premium growth above 8% — with Financial Services at sector PEG 1.1 and HIG at 0.8, any earnings beat triggers institutional rotation from sector average into the quality underwriter; dividend increase announcement is the secondary catalyst. Watch Catastrophe loss activity — an active hurricane season or major commercial property loss event compresses underwriting margins and disrupts the compound story; HIG's geographic concentration in the US Northeast for property exposure is the specific cat risk to monitor.

⚠ Key Risks

  • Fed stays higher-for-longer: if CPI re-accelerates above 3.5%, rate cut expectations evaporate and growth multiples face further compression — most damaging to MNDY and DUOL which rely on multiple normalization as a catalyst
  • Pharma marketing budget risk for DOCS: if GLP-1 drug launches absorb disproportionate pharma ad budgets at the expense of smaller brand spend, DOCS revenue acceleration could stall in Q1-Q2 2026 earnings — the thesis requires pharma spend recovery
  • EM currency and geopolitical risk for DLO: a dollar strengthening cycle or regional FX crises across Nigeria, Argentina, or Egypt could compress DLO's take rates and TPV growth — the 47% revenue growth is impressive but EM macro volatility is the non-negotiable tail risk
🌍 Macro Dashboard — FRED data as of 2026-04-14

AI MARKET SYNOPSIS

The market is simultaneously overpricing macro fear and underpricing business-specific earnings inflections. With the yield curve now positive (+53bps), VIX at 18.4, and unemployment at 4.3%, the economy is not in recession — yet markets have punished high-quality compounders to trough valuations more consistent with a 2008-style crisis. The real opportunity is in consensus picks where four specialists independently converged on the same name: these are not accidents, they are systematic mispricings of durable businesses trading at deeply discounted multiples relative to their cash generation quality.

🌍 GEOPOLITICAL & MACRO CONTEXT

The 10Y at 4.26% and Fed Funds at 3.64% compress growth multiples — this environment structurally favors high-ROIC cash generators over speculative growth stories, which is precisely why QLYS (46% FCF margin), DOCS (49% FCF margin), and HALO (46% FCF margin) are the right quality tier. CPI at 3.29% means the Fed is unlikely to cut aggressively, keeping rates elevated but the normal yield curve (+53bps) and low VIX (18.4) confirm no imminent recession — this is a soft-landing environment where beaten-down quality, not panic, is the right posture.
